title: I'm the most powerful person in Boulder

enrichment.summary: Kyle, recording at 2:46am while high and tired, riffs on the concept of being the 'most powerful person in Boulder' by being a 'Rainmaker' — someone who reads where history is going and stamps their vision on it first. He describes plans to map all Boulder-area mountain bike trails in Google Earth, recreate Roman history through bike rides, and redefine what a 'truly professional' endurance athlete looks like through transparent daily documentation rather than race results.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: The most valuable kind is the historian who sees where things are going, builds the framework first, then stamps their identity on it

Boulder is the closest thing to communism/socialism because the land (trails, mountains) is shared — the poorest person there has a higher quality of life than the richest person somewhere without that infrastructure

Current pro athletes follow an elite-only model; true professionalism means riding all day every day and documenting everything transparently so the data contributes to broader human performance understanding
Building a comprehensive Google Earth trail network for Boulder is the right approach because the native map interfaces on existing trail sites are too slow and limited
We should assume humanity survives a million years and plan for a fully automated future where people spend their days riding bikes and exploring
Listening to history audiobooks on rides — especially ones that match the terrain you're riding through — is the ideal way to learn and engage all your senses simultaneously
